notes_md:
**Quần áo** means *clothes* or *clothing* in general — what you wear on your body.

It’s usually used as an uncountable idea, like “clothes” in English. If you need to count, use **bộ** (outfits/sets) or sometimes **cái** for individual items.  
It’s a fixed pair: you normally don’t separate *quần* and *áo* when you mean “clothes” in general.  
More formal synonym: **trang phục** (used for uniforms, performance outfits, official contexts).
exampleSentencesTranslated: I washed all my clothes at the weekend.
She often buys new clothes whenever she gets paid.
I forgot to bring clothes when staying over at my friend's house.

translation.en: I need to buy more clothes.

example.en: I need to buy new clothes for the summer.
example.es: Necesito comprar ropa nueva para el verano.
example.vi: Tôi cần mua quần áo mới cho mùa hè.

definitions.en: Clothing or garments worn on the body.
definitions.es: Ropa o prendas que se llevan en el cuerpo.
definitions.vi: Trang phục hoặc đồ mặc trên cơ thể.

translation.en: I like to wear new clothes.

exampleSentences: Anh đã giặt hết quần áo của mình vào cuối tuần rồi.
Chị ấy thường mua quần áo mới mỗi khi có lương.
Em quên mang quần áo đi khi đến nhà bạn ở lại.
notes:
Quần áo means clothes or clothing in general — what you wear on your body.

It’s usually used as an uncountable idea, like “clothes” in English. If you need to count, use bộ (outfits/sets) or sometimes cái for individual items.  
It’s a fixed pair: you normally don’t separate quần and áo when you mean “clothes” in general.  
More formal synonym: trang phục (used for uniforms, performance outfits, official contexts).
